query:
INSERT INTO public.questions (
    id, question_text, question_type, options, correct_answer, explanation, hint,
    stimulus_text, stimulus_type, section, topic, difficulty, exam_type,
    is_platform_question, ai_generated, calculator_allowed, subject, subtopic,
    math_domain, pool_key, diagnostic_eligible, tags, external_id, source, source_metadata
  ) VALUES
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"Consequently, the invention of the printing press had a profound impact on society, enabling the spread of ideas and knowledge to a much wider audience.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"Consequently, the invention of the printing press had a profound impact on society, enabling the spread of ideas and knowledge to a much wider audience.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"Consequently, the invention of the printing press had a profound impact on society, enabling the spread of ideas and knowledge to a much wider audience.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"Consequently, the invention of the printing press had a profound impact on society, enabling the spread of ideas and knowledge to a much wider audience.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'Choice A is the best option because it uses a semicolon to properly join the two independent clauses. Choice B uses an incorrect comma, creating a comma splice. Choice C uses an incorrect dash. Choice D uses an incorrect colon, creating a run-on sentence. Therefore, only Choice A conforms to the conventions of Standard English.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The invention of the printing press in the 15th century was a pivotal moment in the history of communication, ushering in a new era of widespread literacy and knowledge dissemination. Before the printing press, the vast majority of books were copied by hand, a process that was both time-consuming and costly. As a result, access to books was limited to a small, privileged few.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'random_id_english_1',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the excerpt?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"It contrasts the protagonist’s attitude at the beginning of the novel with the protagonist’s attitude at the end of the novel.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"It provides an example of how the protagonist’s attitude changes over the course of the novel.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"It explains the reasons why the protagonist’s attitude changes over the course of the novel.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"It introduces the idea that the protagonist’s attitude changes over the course of the novel.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The underlined sentence directly contrasts the protagonist’s attitude at the beginning of the novel with their attitude at the end of the novel.  The author first states that the protagonist is cynical and pessimistic, and then goes on to say that the protagonist begins to see the world in a different light.  This contrast in attitude is the main point of the underlined sentence.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author’s purpose in this excerpt is to describe the way in which the protagonist’s attitude changes over the course of the novel.  At the beginning of the novel, the protagonist is a very cynical and pessimistic person.  They do not believe in love, and they do not believe in happiness.  However, as the novel progresses, the protagonist begins to see the world in a different light.  They begin to believe that there is beauty in the world, and they begin to believe in the possibility of love.  This change in attitude is reflected in the way that the protagonist speaks and in the way that the protagonist interacts with other characters.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Craft and Structure']::text[],
    'random_id_e8',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Craft and Structure","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which of the following choices corrects the punctuation errors in the underlined portion of the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"and her husband, Leonard Woolf; and E.M. Forster; and painter Vanessa Bell.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"and her husband, Leonard Woolf, and E.M. Forster, and painter Vanessa Bell.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"and her husband, Leonard Woolf; and E.M. Forster; and, painter Vanessa Bell.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"and her husband, Leonard Woolf, and E.M. Forster, and painter Vanessa Bell","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The correct way to punctuate a series of three or more items is to use a comma after each item except the last.  A comma should precede the conjunction "and" before the last item.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'In the early twentieth century, an influential group of artists  known as the Bloomsbury Group, often gathered in London to discuss art, literature, and politics. The group included writers like Virginia Woolf,  and her husband Leonard Woolf;  and E.M. Forster; and painter Vanessa Bell.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'random_id_c2',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'In this novel, the setting is a rural town primarily to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"create a sense of isolation and loneliness for the protagonist.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"provide a backdrop for the protagonist’s artistic development.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"highlight the contrast between urban and rural life.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"explore the challenges of finding one’s place in a small community.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'The rural setting is used to emphasize the challenges Ella faces in finding her place in a small community.  The limited opportunities and rigid social structures of a rural town can make it difficult for someone like Ella to pursue their dreams and find their own path in life.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The novel follows the protagonist, a young woman named Ella, as she navigates the complexities of her life in a rural town.  Ella is a talented musician, but she struggles to find her place in a world that seems to have little use for her artistic aspirations.  Her journey is marked by both joy and heartbreak, as she grapples with family relationships, societal expectations, and her own sense of identity.  What is the most likely reason why the writer chose to set the novel in a rural town?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'random_id_c1',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice provides the best way to combine the sentences at the end of the first paragraph to create a smoother flow?  

The composer’s research progressed, he discovered that the pine trees of Rome had a long and interesting history, and he decided to incorporate this history into the composition.  Making the poem a reflection of both the beauty of the pine trees and their cultural significance.',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The composer’s research progressed; he discovered that the pine trees of Rome had a long and interesting history, and he decided to incorporate this history into the composition, making the poem a reflection of both the beauty of the pine trees and their cultural significance.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The composer’s research progressed, he discovered that the pine trees of Rome had a long and interesting history, and he decided to incorporate this history into the composition, making the poem a reflection of both the beauty of the pine trees and their cultural significance.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The composer’s research progressed, he discovered that the pine trees of Rome had a long and interesting history; he decided to incorporate this history into the composition, making the poem a reflection of both the beauty of the pine trees and their cultural significance.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The composer’s research progressed, he discovered that the pine trees of Rome had a long and interesting history, and he decided to incorporate this history into the composition, making the poem a reflection of both the beauty of the pine trees and their cultural significance.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'Choice C is the best way to combine the sentences. The semicolon helps to separate the two independent clauses.  It also creates a smoother flow between the two parts of the sentence.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'In 1925, the Italian composer Ottorino Respighi began working on an orchestral work, the tone poem *The Pines of Rome,* which would become one of his most famous compositions.  The composition was inspired by Respighi’s early travels to Rome, where he was enchanted by the city’s pine trees, which were common in the region’s parks.  As the composer’s research progressed, he discovered that the pine trees of Rome had a long and interesting history, and he decided to incorporate this history into the composition, making the poem a reflection of both the beauty of the pine trees and their cultural significance.  Respighi’s four movements, which are based on distinct phases in the life of the pine trees, include a depiction of the pine trees as they appear in the Roman countryside, their emergence as a symbol of the city’s power and growth, the way they are used in the city’s gardens and parks, and finally, their ultimate transformation as they become a symbol of the city’s past.  Respighi’s composition has been praised by music critics for its rich musical language, its vivid depiction of the pine trees, and its emotional power.  The work has been performed by orchestras around the world since it was first performed in 1924.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'f765438a',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'In this passage, the phrase "all been born long ago" is used to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"emphasize the speaker’s feelings and experiences.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"suggest that the trees are ancient and venerable.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"create a sense of intimacy and connection with the reader.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"describe the speaker’s sense of wonder and awe.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The phrase "all been born long ago" is used to emphasize the age and longevity of the trees.  This makes the trees seem ancient and venerable, which would be a sign of their importance in the passage.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The speaker notes that the trees had "all been born long ago."   This phrase suggests that the trees were alive before any of the three viewers were born.   What is the most likely reason why the writer chose the phrase "all been born long ago"?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'a149f288',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'What does the study by Dr. Smith’s team suggest about the brain''s capacity to form new memories?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The brain''s capacity to form new memories is a fixed trait, and regular engagement in mentally challenging activities does not change this capacity.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The brain''s capacity to form new memories is influenced by a person''s age, and older adults are less likely to form new memories than younger individuals.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The brain''s capacity to form new memories is influenced by a person''s level of mental activity, and regular engagement in mentally challenging activities may help to preserve this capacity.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The brain''s capacity to form new memories is influenced by a person''s environment, and those who live in stimulating environments are more likely to form new memories than those who live in less stimulating environments.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The study suggests that the brain''s capacity to form new memories is not solely dependent on age, but rather is influenced by a person''s level of mental activity. Regular engagement in intellectually demanding activities may help to preserve this capacity.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'While the study by researchers led by Dr. Smith, published in the "Journal of Psychological Research," showed that the brain''s capacity to form new memories does not decrease with age, they also discovered that this capacity may be linked to the number of times a person engages in mentally challenging activities.   Dr. Smith’s team found that older adults who regularly engaged in intellectually demanding activities, such as playing games, reading complex texts, and solving puzzles, had brains that were just as capable of forming new memories as those of much younger individuals. However, older adults who rarely or never engaged in such activities showed a noticeable decrease in their brain’s ability to form new memories.  The study’s findings suggest that ______',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'random_id_c7',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best revises the underlined portion to create a more concise and precise sentence?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"has been adapted into a film, and it has become a classic of American literature.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"has been adapted into a film, becoming a classic of American literature.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"has been adapted into a film; it has also become a classic of American literature.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"became a film and has become a classic of American literature.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'Choice B is the most concise and precise revision because it combines the two independent clauses, "The novel has been adapted into a film" and "The novel has become a classic of American literature," into a single, clear sentence. It avoids unnecessary repetition and redundancy. Choice A uses a comma splice to separate two independent clauses. Choice C uses a semicolon to separate two independent clauses, which is not the preferred way to combine these two thoughts. Choice D is incorrect because it uses the past tense verb "became" to describe a state of being that is ongoing, while the text is about a state of being that is generally true. ',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The 1995 novel "The House of Mirth" by Edith Wharton, a novel that focuses on the life of Lily Bart, a young woman who lives in New York City, has been praised for its sharp social commentary and realistic depiction of turn-of-the-century society, as well as for its exploration of themes such as class, gender, and morality.  The novel has also been adapted into a film and has become a classic of American literature.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'random_id_c9',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best combines the sentences at the underlined portion so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The study’s findings have led researchers to believe that physical activity may have a positive effect on quality of life.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The study’s findings have led researchers to believe: physical activity may have a positive effect on quality of life.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The study’s findings have led researchers to believe that physical activity may have a positive effect; on quality of life.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The study’s findings have led researchers to believe that physical activity may have a positive effect, on quality of life.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'Choice A is the best answer because it combines the two sentences correctly without creating any punctuation errors. The other choices create punctuation errors; choice B uses a colon incorrectly, choice C uses a semicolon incorrectly, and choice D uses a comma incorrectly.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'A large study recently revealed that those individuals who frequently engage in physical activity report a higher quality of life than those who do not.  The study’s findings have led researchers to believe that physical activity may have a positive effect on quality of life.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'e150a912',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best corrects a grammatical error in the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The author of the novel *The Great Gatsby* is F. Scott Fitzgerald, who was born in Saint Paul, Minnesota, but later moved to the East Coast where he was a student at Princeton University, and a well-known writer.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The author of the novel *The Great Gatsby* is F. Scott Fitzgerald, who was born in Saint Paul, Minnesota, but later moved to the East Coast where he was a student at Princeton University, a well-known writer.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The author of the novel *The Great Gatsby* is F. Scott Fitzgerald, who was born in Saint Paul, Minnesota, but later moved to the East Coast where he was a student at Princeton University and later, a well-known writer.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The author of the novel *The Great Gatsby* is F. Scott Fitzgerald, who was born in Saint Paul, Minnesota, but later moved to the East Coast, where he was a student at Princeton University, and a well-known writer.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'The original sentence is a comma splice, two independent clauses joined by a comma.  To correct this, we need to use a semicolon or a coordinating conjunction (and, but, or, nor, for, so, yet) or to make the sentence into two separate sentences. Choice D correctly uses a comma to separate two independent clauses that are joined by a coordinating conjunction. Choice A is incorrect because it creates a comma splice. Choice B is incorrect because it creates a comma splice.  Choice C is incorrect because it creates a comma splice and the phrase "and later" is misplaced. ',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author of the novel *The Great Gatsby* is F. Scott Fitzgerald, who was born in Saint Paul, Minnesota, but later moved to the East Coast where he was a student at Princeton University, and later, a well-known writer.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    '34d9f783',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author’s organization of the passage is most likely intended to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"explain how the sport has changed over time.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"highlight the sport’s historical significance.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"compare the sport’s past popularity to its present popularity.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"argue that the sport’s popularity is a result of its modern-day innovations.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The passage traces the development of the sport from its earliest known uses to its modern-day popularity. This chronological structure helps the reader understand how the sport has changed over time.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author writes about the history of a particular sport, starting with the earliest known uses of the sport. The author then moves on to describe the evolution of the sport, including the development of new techniques and the use of new equipment. Finally, the author discusses the sport’s modern-day popularity and the impact it has had on society. What is the most likely reason why the author structures the passage in this way?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Craft and Structure']::text[],
    'random_id_a1',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Craft and Structure","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The sentence "Although she had been practicing for weeks, the dancer''s first performance was awkward and unpolished."  Which choice provides the best punctuation and capitalization of the underlined portion?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"Although she had been practicing for weeks, the Dancers first performance was awkward and unpolished.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"Although she had been practicing for weeks, the dancer''s first performance was awkward and unpolished.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"Although she had been practicing for weeks, the dancer''s first performance was awkward, and unpolished.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"Although she had been practicing for weeks the dancer''s first performance was awkward and unpolished.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The sentence is grammatically correct. The only change needed is to capitalize the "D" in "Dancer''s" because it is a proper noun. The punctuation is correct as is, with a comma after the introductory phrase "Although she had been practicing for weeks."',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    NULL,
    NULL,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,

    NULL,

    'random_id_e8',
    'college_board_opensat',

  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'In this passage, the author''s use of sentence fragments most likely serves to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"add a touch of informality to the passage.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"create a sense of excitement and urgency.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"highlight the speaker''s sense of confusion.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"emphasize the speaker''s lack of education.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'Sentence fragments can be used to create a sense of excitement and urgency, as they can make the writing feel more immediate and impactful. This is consistent with the passage''s discussion of the speaker''s excitement and enthusiasm.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author''s use of sentence fragments creates a sense of immediacy and urgency. This technique is effective in conveying the speaker''s excitement and enthusiasm. However, some readers may find the abruptness of the sentence fragments to be jarring.  What is the most likely reason the author chooses to use sentence fragments?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,

  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'In this passage, the focus on the sweetgrass is intended to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"contrast the sweetgrass’s beauty with the look of a factory-made basket.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"explain the process of how the sweetgrass is woven into the basket.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"highlight the significance of the sweetgrass to the basket’s unique design.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"suggest that the sweetgrass is the most valuable part of the basket.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The passage focuses on the sweetgrass because it is what makes the basket unique. The author uses language to describe the intricate process and highlights the sweetgrass as the key element in the basket''s design, emphasizing its significance.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author emphasizes the importance of the "sweetgrass" in the basket through the use of vivid and specific language. The author uses words like "skillfully winds" and "signature look" to create an image of the sweetgrass’s beauty and importance. What is the most likely reason why the author chose to focus on the importance of the sweetgrass in the basket?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,

    'multiple_choice',

","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The research team, which is made up of scientists from several universities, discovered a new species of butterfly in the Amazon rainforest, the scientists named the species *Amazonia elegans*. ","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The research team, which is made up of scientists from several universities, discovered a new species of butterfly in the Amazon rainforest, and the scientists named the species *Amazonia elegans*. ","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The research team, which is made up of scientists from several universities, discovered a new species of butterfly in the Amazon rainforest and the scientists named the species *Amazonia elegans*. ","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The best way to combine the sentences is by using a comma and a coordinating conjunction to link the two independent clauses. Choice A creates a comma splice, and choices B and D are incorrect because they don’t include a coordinating conjunction.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The passage suggests that',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"learning by doing is the best way to learn about new ideas.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"scientists have found that learning by doing is more effective than learning by reading.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"learning by doing allows people to experience the idea firsthand, which can help them to understand it more deeply.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"students who learn about the human body by reading a textbook may not understand the complex interactions of the different systems as well as a student who dissects a frog.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The main point of the passage is that learning by doing is the best way to learn about new ideas. The author supports this point by explaining that learning by doing allows people to experience the idea firsthand, which can help them to understand it more deeply. The author then provides an example to illustrate this point: a student who learns about the human body by reading a textbook may not understand the complex interactions of the different systems as well as a student who dissects a frog. This example highlights the key point of the passage: learning by doing is the best way to learn about new ideas.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'Many people believe that the best way to understand a new idea is to read about it in a book or article.  However, scientists have found that learning by doing can be more effective than learning by reading.  This is because learning by doing allows people to experience the idea firsthand, which can help them to understand it more deeply. For example, a student who learns about the human body by reading a textbook may not understand the complex interactions of the different systems as well as a student who dissects a frog.  What is the main point of the passage?',

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"It introduces a contrast between Dickinson’s personality and her writing style.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"It clarifies the reason why Dickinson’s writing was often marked by a sense of isolation.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"It provides a specific example of Dickinson’s writing that is often marked by a sense of isolation.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"It suggests a connection between Dickinson’s decision to withdraw from society and her ability to experience deep emotion.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'The underlined sentence connects Dickinson’s decision to withdraw from society, which the author describes as a choice to live “a life of quiet contemplation,” to her ability to experience deep emotion. Although Dickinson’s writing is often marked by a sense of isolation, she was also capable of deep emotion. This connection is implied by the author’s use of the word “although,” which suggests a contrast between Dickinson’s writing style and her capacity for deep emotion. The author’s assertion that Dickinson’s writing was often marked by a sense of isolation may seem contradictory, but the author clarifies that Dickinson was also capable of deep emotion. This suggests that Dickinson’s isolation was not due to a lack of emotion but rather a choice that allowed her to focus on her art. The author is linking Dickinson’s writing style to her personality, so the underlined sentence is connecting a character trait to an action.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author describes the character of Emily Dickinson as “a recluse” who “lived a life of quiet contemplation.”  Dickinson’s decision to withdraw from society is further emphasized by the author’s use of the word “secluded.”  The author then goes on to say that Dickinson “chose to be a poet, and a poet only.”  In the last sentence of the passage, the author explains that although “Dickinson’s writing was often marked by a sense of isolation,” “she was also capable of deep emotion.” What is the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,

    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The Industrial Revolution began in Great Britain, but it quickly spread throughout the world, affecting economies and societies in Asia, Africa, the Americas, and Oceania. ","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The Industrial Revolution was not merely an economic phenomenon, but a social and cultural one as well, and that its effects are still felt today. ","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The Industrial Revolution transformed the way people lived, worked, and interacted with each other, and these transformations continue to shape the world we live in today.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"It is important to remember that the Industrial Revolution wasn’t merely an economic phenomenon, but a social and cultural one as well, and that its effects are still felt today. ","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The passage emphasizes that the Industrial Revolution transformed the way people lived, worked, and interacted with each other. This is the passage’s central argument and is supported by the other claims.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'One of the most common misunderstandings about the Industrial Revolution is that it only affected Europe. While the Industrial Revolution began in Great Britain, it quickly spread throughout the world, affecting economies and societies in Asia, Africa, the Americas, and Oceania. It’s important to remember that the Industrial Revolution wasn’t merely an economic phenomenon, but a social and cultural one as well, and that its effects are still felt today. The Industrial Revolution transformed the way people lived, worked, and interacted with each other, and these transformations continue to shape the world we live in today.',

    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"Tolkien, a philologist who created a world rich in mythology, history, and language, crafted a system of languages that were intricately connected and that were used to give his characters a sense of authenticity.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"Tolkien, a philologist, created a world rich in mythology, history, and language, and crafted a system of languages that were intricately connected and that were used to give his characters a sense of authenticity.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"Tolkien, a philologist and created a world rich in mythology, history, and language, crafted a system of languages that were intricately connected and that were used to give his characters a sense of authenticity.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"Tolkien, a philologist, created a world rich in mythology, history, and language, crafted a system of languages that were intricately connected and that were used to give his characters a sense of authenticity.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The underlined portion has a comma splice, which is an error in Standard English. To correct this error, the comma should be replaced with a semicolon or a conjunction. Choice B is the best option because the semicolon correctly separates the two independent clauses. Choices A and C are incorrect because they create a comma splice. Choice D is incorrect because it creates a run-on sentence.',

    'The novel *The Lord of the Rings* by J. R. R. Tolkien is one of the most beloved works of fantasy literature. Tolkien, a philologist, created a world rich in mythology, history, and language.     'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The word \"serendipity\" was once used only to describe a lucky or fortunate event, but it has now been extended to include unexpected discoveries.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The word \"serendipity\" has evolved to encompass both expected and unexpected discoveries, but it’s no longer used in casual conversations.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The word \"serendipity\" is often used to describe a lucky or fortunate event, but it is no longer limited to monumental events like inventions and discoveries.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The word \"serendipity\" has been used to describe accidental inventions and discoveries for many years, but it has only recently become popular in casual conversations.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The main idea of this passage is that the word "serendipity" is often used to describe a lucky or fortunate event, but it is no longer limited to monumental events like inventions and discoveries. Choices A, B, and D are incorrect because they do not accurately reflect the main idea.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The sentence "The cat sat on the mat, and then it ate the mouse." contains a comma splice. Which of the following revisions corrects the comma splice? ',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The cat sat on the mat and then it ate the mouse.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The cat sat on the mat, it then ate the mouse.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The cat sat on the mat, and then it ate the mouse.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The cat sat on the mat then it ate the mouse.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'A comma splice occurs when two independent clauses are joined together with only a comma. To correct a comma splice, you can either use a semicolon, a coordinating conjunction (and, but, or, nor, for, so, yet), or separate the two sentences with a period. Choice A corrects the comma splice by using a coordinating conjunction. Choice B is incorrect because it doesn’t separate the two clauses with a coordinating conjunction, semicolon, or period. Choice C is incorrect because it is the original sentence with the comma splice. Choice D is incorrect because it is a run-on sentence, where two sentences are joined together without any punctuation.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',

    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"which suggests that mindfulness can have a positive impact on mental health, offering a way for people to manage the challenges of daily life.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"offering a way for people to manage the challenges of daily life, and their results suggest that mindfulness can have a positive impact on mental health.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"and their results suggest that mindfulness can have a positive impact on mental health, offering a way for people to manage the challenges of daily life.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":", their results suggest that mindfulness can have a positive impact on mental health, offering a way for people to manage the challenges of daily life.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'Choice A is the best option because it combines the sentences with a comma and a relative clause, creating a concise and grammatically correct sentence that maintains the formal style of the passage.     'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',

    'Which choice best combines the sentences at the underlined portion to maintain the style and tone of the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"mesmerizing, but also unusual, and the dancing fish’s movements are thought to be a way for the fish to attract mates.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"mesmerizing, but also unusual, and scientists think that the dancing fish’s movements are a way for the fish to attract mates.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"mesmerizing, but also unusual, and scientists would like to study them in a lab setting to control for other variables that could affect the fish''s behavior, such as the availability of food and the presence of predators.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"mesmerizing, but also unusual, scientists think that the dancing fish’s movements are a way for the fish to attract mates, and scientists would like to study them in a lab setting to control for other variables that could affect the fish''s behavior, such as the availability of food and the presence of predators.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'Choice B is the most logical and concise way to combine the sentences.  The phrase "and scientists think that" maintains the style and tone of the passage, creating a smooth flow between the two ideas.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The recent discovery of a new species of fish—the so-called "dancing fish"—has scientists excited because the fish’s unique dancing movements are not just mesmerizing, but also unusual. The dancing fish’s movements are thought to be a way for the fish to attract mates.  While the dancing fish''s movements have been studied in the wild, scientists would like to study them in a lab setting to control for other variables that could affect the fish''s behavior, such as the availability of food and the presence of predators.',

    'B',
    'The author argues that history is a complex and dynamic process, not simply a collection of facts.  The author is arguing that social, economic, and cultural forces have shaped the past in a way that is more than just a series of events.  The author is trying to challenge the reader''s traditional view of history and help them see the past as a dynamic process.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The essay argues that the traditional view of history as a series of events is flawed. The author suggests that it is important to consider the larger context of the past, including the social, economic, and cultural forces that have shaped it. In this view, history is not simply a collection of facts, but a complex and dynamic process of change and transformation. The essay challenges readers to think critically about history and to see the past not as a static thing but as a living and evolving force.  The essay argues that the traditional view of history is flawed. The author suggests that it is important to consider the larger context of the past, including the social, economic, and cultural forces that have shaped it. In this view, history is not simply a collection of facts, but a complex and dynamic process of change and transformation. The essay challenges readers to think critically about history and to see the past not as a static thing but as a living and evolving force.  What is the most likely reason why the author cites social, economic, and cultural forces as important elements of the past?',
